primary_factors: Primary hazard is a rain-to-freeze (flash-freeze/black ice) window near bus time; snowfall accumulation is essentially nil
Timing is borderline: temperatures cross ~32°F around ~7 AM with gusty winds, increasing risk of rapid icing on bridge decks, ramps, and untreated neighborhood streets
Visibility reductions (fog/low stratus) around the morning window can compound crash risk even if pavement is only patchy-slick
No confirmed neighboring-district closures and no Rockford PS announcement yet—weak “domino effect”/community-pressure signal at this time
Power outage risk appears localized/pockety (not a widespread grid failure signal), but any school-facility outage would be a high-leverage closure trigger
decision_rationale: All experts broadly agree this is not a snow-accumulation event; it’s a flash-freeze/black-ice risk morning. Meteorology and web verification show no extreme-cold trigger (no ≤ -20°F wind chills) and near-zero snow totals, which in Michigan usually means schools try to run. History supports a low-to-moderate closure base rate for “rain then sharp freeze by commute,” with delays more common than full cancellations unless there is verified glazing/freezing drizzle or widespread early slide-offs. Safety is the outlier (higher) due to tail-risk: if surfaces stay wet into the 32°F crossing, slickness can become widespread quickly—especially on secondary roads and neighborhood pickups. News intel currently shows no Kent-area closure wave and no Rockford posting, which keeps the closure probability below the 40–60% band. Infrastructure confidence is low, but the most consistent operational logic is: main roads likely manageable with treatment; the risk is patchy-but-dangerous on bridges and local streets plus reduced visibility. Net: a full closure is possible but less likely than a 1–2 hour delay; I set closure probability at 27% to reflect meaningful but not dominant flash-freeze risk while acknowledging Michigan’s high bar and the lack of domino/announcement signals.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 15.0% spread. Average probability: 25%.
